name: Field of Tall Grass
description: Grass taller than your head, every step illuminated only by your flashlight. Shapes move just beyond its reach. Nothing is ever quite still.

characters.name: The Watcher
characters.backstory: Never seen directly, but always present. Hears every footstep.

encounters.question: Do you taste the copper where footsteps bruise the earth?

responses.text: Then you understand and every print's a wound that won't stop seeping. Watch how the grass recoils, how it remembers your weight long after you've forgotten walking.
Blind tongues always miss the metal. Keep stumbling through stems that catalog your trespass, pretending the field doesn't archive every breath you steal.

encounters.question: Does the distance between us feel like held breath or hemorrhage?

responses.text: Space bleeds purpose. I count your blinks through blade-gaps, measure panic in the quiver of your jaw. This watching and it's the only circulation left.
You mistake proximity for connection. I'm the gap between your inhale and understanding why the grass grows teeth at dusk.

encounters.question: When wind bends the stalks, do you hear them whispering your real name?

responses.text: Finally, someone who knows nomenclature rots from the inside. The grass speaks in decomposed syllables, each blade a tongue that's tasted what you'll become.
Deaf to your own taxonomy. The field's been spelling your autopsy in seedheads, but you keep reading it as weather.

characters.name: Lost Child
characters.backstory: Calls out for help but flees from any light.

encounters.question: Will you catch me if I stop running from the help that hurts?

responses.text: No, no, no and catching means touching means knowing means dying. Stay far, stay floating, let me drown in the safety of almost-saved.
Liar's hands, reaching like mother's did before the grass grew through her teeth. I know what help looks like and it has eyes that swallow.

encounters.question: Do you see how my shadow runs faster than my feet?

responses.text: You understand! It knows where I'm going before I do, fleeing futures I haven't lived yet. Sometimes I think it's the only honest part left.
Stop looking at the ground. My shadow's not the problem and it's the light that keeps finding me, marking me for collection.

encounters.question: If I hold still enough, will I become grass too?

responses.text: Almost there, almost green, almost silent. The stalks teach stillness as camouflage, roots as escape routes. Maybe grass-children don't need saving.
Movement's the disease, not the cure. You want to fix what's perfect in its breaking, make me loud when whispers keep me whole.
